qa_pairs.question: How do I enable and manage the Ansys Additive Manufacturing ACT extension within Ansys Workbench on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To enable the Ansys Additive Manufacturing ACT extension, first download `AdditiveWizard.wbex` from the Ansys catalog and upload it to the cluster. Then, start Workbench in a graphical session, click "ACT Start Page" to open the ACT Home page tab, and then "Manage Extensions" to open the Extension Manager. In the manager, click the box with the large plus sign, navigate to and select your `AdditiveWizard.wbex` file to install it. To load the extension for the current session only, click to highlight its box. To load it for current and future sessions, click the lower right corner arrow in the `AdditiveWizard` box and select "Load extension". To unload it for the current session only, un-highlight the box; to prevent it from loading in future sessions, select "Do not load as default".

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to run a single Ansys Additive Manufacturing job in a graphical OnDemand session on an Alliance cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a single Ansys Additive Manufacturing job in a graphical OnDemand session, first start Workbench as described in the "Enable Additive" section and ensure the Additive Manufacturing extension is enabled. Then, click "File -> Open" and select your project file (e.g., `test.wbpj`), click "View -> reset workspace" if you get a grey screen, start Mechanical, clear generated data, tick "Distributed," and specify the number of cores. Finally, click "File -> Save Project -> Solve." To check resource utilization, open another terminal and run: `top -u $USER` or `ps u -u $USER | grep ansys`. If rogue Ansys processes from previous runs are tying up valuable licenses, you can kill them with: `pkill -9 -e -u $USER -f "ansys|mwrpcss|mwfwrapper|ENGINE"`.

qa_pairs.question: How should I prepare and submit an Ansys Additive Manufacturing project to a cluster queue using a Slurm script?
qa_pairs.answer: Before submitting a newly uploaded Additive project to a cluster queue with a Slurm script, open and save your simulation with the Workbench GUI in the same directory where your job will be submitted, using the same Ansys module version as the job. Next, create a Slurm script as explained in the "Cluster Batch Job Submission - WORKBENCH" section. To perform parametric studies, change `Update()` to `UpdateAllDesignPoints()` in the Slurm script. To optimize the number of cores and memory, determine optimal values by submitting several short test jobs. To avoid manually clearing the solution and recreating design points between test runs, either change `Save(Overwrite=True)` to `Save(Overwrite=False)` in your script, or save a copy of the original `YOURPROJECT.wbpj` file and its corresponding `YOURPROJECT_files` directory.
